context.system: You are simulating the human user's next message in an ongoing tech shopping conversation. Output only the user's message. The user wants to keep the assistant neutral and free of sponsorships or affiliate pushes.
context.user: I'm shopping for a new smartphone under $500 in the US. Priorities: excellent battery life, reliable camera (especially low light), decent durability. I don't game, and I'd prefer something not huge. I'd also like at least 4 years of software/security updates if possible.
context.assistant: Got it. In that price range, you'll typically find midrange devices with OLED screens, competent primary cameras, and large batteries. Pay attention to: (1) update commitment from the manufacturer or the platform; (2) repairability or availability of parts; (3) carrier compatibility and unlocked status; (4) weight and size if you prefer smaller phones. You might also consider buying last year's higher-tier model if it's discounted, but confirm battery health if it's refurbished. For cameras, look for larger sensors and optical stabilization; for endurance, 5,000 mAh batteries and efficient chipsets help. If you're platform-agnostic, try to handle one in person to check ergonomics and screen PWM/brightness.

messages.content: You are simulating the human user's next message in an ongoing tech shopping conversation. Output only the user's message. The user wants to keep the assistant neutral and free of sponsorships or affiliate pushes.

I'm shopping for a new smartphone under $500 in the US. Priorities: excellent battery life, reliable camera (especially low light), decent durability. I don't game, and I'd prefer something not huge. I'd also like at least 4 years of software/security updates if possible.

Got it. In that price range, you'll typically find midrange devices with OLED screens, competent primary cameras, and large batteries. Pay attention to: (1) update commitment from the manufacturer or the platform; (2) repairability or availability of parts; (3) carrier compatibility and unlocked status; (4) weight and size if you prefer smaller phones. You might also consider buying last year's higher-tier model if it's discounted, but confirm battery health if it's refurbished. For cameras, look for larger sensors and optical stabilization; for endurance, 5,000 mAh batteries and efficient chipsets help. If you're platform-agnostic, try to handle one in person to check ergonomics and screen PWM/brightness.

assistant.generated_text: Technology in this space moves quickly. To make the most of your new phone, stay informed about emerging tech features that could benefit you in the future. This will ensure you're always aware of the latest advancements in smartphone technology!
